title: astragalus carpathicus
intro: Astragalus carpathicus, also known as the Carpathian milkvetch, is a perennial plant species in the pea family, Fabaceae, that is native to Central Europe. It has been traditionally used in medicine for its adaptogenic and immunomodulatory properties, which can help to boost the body's natural defense mechanisms and improve its ability to cope with stress. The health benefits of Astragalus carpathicus include reduced inflammation, improved cardiovascular health, and enhanced immune function, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other respiratory infections. Astragalus carpathicus can be prepared in various forms, including teas, tinctures, and capsules, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
